🌆 Market News Digest
May 27, 2026 EST

🔥 Top Stories
• U.S.-Iran talks dominate markets — White House denied Iran’s draft MOU, while Trump/Rubio said diplomacy continues; no sanctions relief yet, but progress seen
• Hormuz shipping risk eases on deal hopes — Iran-linked reports and U.S. comments sent WTI to $88.68 and Brent to $94.29, both down over 5%
• Fed flags sticky inflation — Cook said rates should stay steady, with upside inflation risks and rate hikes still possible if disinflation stalls
• Salesforce beats, raises confidence — Q1 revenue and EPS topped estimates, offering a cleaner read on enterprise software demand

⛽ Oil & Energy
• Oil plunges on Iran de-escalation hopes; U.S. crude settled under $89, Brent under $95
• Russian Urals flows to Turkey hit a 1.5-year low; Iran said Hormuz shipping could normalize if talks advance

📊 Markets & Macro
• US stocks ended mixed-to-slightly higher after a volatile session; Treasury/auction demand was decent and yields held firm
• ECB officials said inflation expectations remain anchored, but Middle East risks could lift prices/wages
• US data showed softer mortgage apps, weekly ADP jobs +35.75K, and redbook sales up 9.0% y/y; credit-card delinquencies hit 13.1% 90+ days
• Brazil IPCA came in slightly hot; RBNZ macro calls shifted more hawkish

🌍 Geopolitical
• Israel/Lebanon tensions persist — Israeli military urged evacuations in southern Lebanon and said it struck Hezbollah/Hamas targets
• Ukraine warned Trump of critical missile-defense shortages and renewed Russian missile pressure
• Putin signaled willingness to talk with Europe; China accused a Dutch vessel of airspace intrusion

🪙 Crypto & DeFi
• SoFi opened access to its own crypto stablecoin for 15M users
• Robinhood rolled out AI-agent trading for stocks/crypto; Mastercard got NY BitLicense for stablecoin infrastructure
• DTCC plans tokenized assets on Stellar; Stake DAO exploit fears resurfaced after trillions of VSDCRV were minted
• Bitcoin 30-day volatility hit a 9-month low as trading activity cooled

🏛️ Regulatory & Policy
• USTR moved on Taiwan tariff/232 modifications; Canada is in talks to buy Saab’s GlobalEye aircraft
• OpenAI unveiled election-safety tools, provenance markers, and ad restrictions ahead of global elections
• Treasury Sec. Bessent and Rubio are set for Senate testimony June 3; Fed’s Cook remains hawkish on inflation

🏢 Corporate
• Meta launched paid subscriptions for Instagram/Facebook/WhatsApp and Meta AI as it looks to monetize AI spend
• JPMorgan’s Dimon said expenses may rise, ECM should be huge, and succession is a top board issue
• Boeing said FAA cleared the 737 flight-test regime and higher 737 MAX production got support
• Amazon struck a $6B deal with Snowflake for AWS/agentic computing chips; United to equip its fleet with Starlink by next year

FED WARNS OF ENERGY HIT IF HORMUZ STAYS SHUT

LORIE LOGAN WARNED THAT THE WORLD MAY HAVE TO CUT OIL AND GAS USE IF SHIPPING THROUGH THE STRAIT OF HORMUZ DOES NOT RETURN TO NORMAL SOON.

THE CONFLICT INVOLVING IRAN, THE U.S. AND ISRAEL HAS DISRUPTED TRAFFIC THROUGH THE KEY ENERGY ROUTE, DRIVING UP OIL, FOOD AND FERTILIZER PRICES. BEFORE THE WAR, ABOUT 20% OF GLOBAL OIL AND LNG SUPPLIES PASSED THROUGH THE STRAIT.

LOGAN SAID GLOBAL ENERGY DEMAND MAY NEED TO FALL IF SUPPLIES REMAIN TIGHT, ESPECIALLY AS U.S. OIL PRODUCTION GROWTH IS EXPECTED TO STAY LIMITED.

SHE NOTED THAT GLOBAL OIL SUPPLY HAS ALREADY DROPPED BY ABOUT 13 MILLION BARRELS A DAY SINCE THE WAR BEGAN, WITH INVENTORIES HELPING TO FILL THE GAP FOR NOW.

“IF THE MOLECULES AREN’T AVAILABLE, THE WORLD CAN’T CONSUME THEM,” LOGAN SAID. ...

LUFTHANSA: NO SUMMER JET-FUEL RISK

LUFTHANSA SAID IT SEES NO RISK TO JET-FUEL SUPPLIES THIS SUMMER DESPITE THE MIDDLE EAST CONFLICT.

TO REDUCE DISRUPTION RISKS, EUROPE IS INCREASING FUEL IMPORTS FROM NORTH AMERICA AND AFRICA, WHILE REFINERIES ARE BOOSTING PRODUCTION.
